revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Inari Daichi Kofun]][[ja:稲荷台1号墳]] {{Multiple issues | refimprove = October 17, 2022 | citation style = October 17, 2022 }} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Inaridai No.1 Kofun | location = {{ill|Ichihara|qid=Q207881|1=Ichihara, Chiba}} | image = File:Inaridai Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | caption = Restored mound (1/3 map_size) | coordinates = {{Coord|35.50333333|N|140.12397222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | built = Mid-5th century | Historic Site = None | cultural property = "Ōshi" inscribed iron sword
Designated as a municipal {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901860|lt=tangible cultural property|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} of {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara, Chiba|1=Ichihara, Chiba}} on May 2, 2016 | burial = {{ill|Direct wooden coffin burial|ja|木棺直葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Direct wooden coffin burial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳|1=Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1141225}} | burial goods = Central coffin: "Ōshi" inscribed iron sword and two other iron swords, one cuirass, ten iron arrowheads, and one knife
North coffin: One iron sword, ten iron arrowheads, a quiver fitting set, a chisel-shaped fitting, and one whetstone{{Cite web|和書|author=市原市埋蔵文化財調査センター|url=https://www.city.ichihara.chiba.jp/maibun/isekifile27.htm|title=稲荷台古墳群|work=市原市|accessdate=2020-05-19}} | type = {{ill|Circular tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 28 meters | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Inaridai
No.1 Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=30|coordinates=12.0|lon_deg=140|lon_min=07|lon_sec=26.3|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|12.0|N|140|07|26.3|E}}}} | part_of = {{ill|Inaridai Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11596849|1=Inaridai Kofun Group}} }} '''Inaridai No.1 Kofun''' (稲荷台1号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} that types part of the {{ill|Inaridai Kofun Group|en|Inaridai Kofun Group|qid=Q11596849|lt=Inaridai Kofun Group|1=Inaridai Kofun Group}} located on the northern terrace of the lower {{ill|Yōrō River|en|Yōrō River|qid=Q8062667|lt=Yōrō River|1=Yōrō River}} in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県市原市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihara, Chiba Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A {{ill|Damascening|en|Damascening|qid=Q2295879|lt=silver inlay|1=Damascening}} inscription iron sword, Historic Site1 a municipal {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901860|lt=tangible cultural property|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}, was excavated from this site.{{Cite web|和書|author=生涯学習部ふるさと文化課|url=https://www.city.ichihara.chiba.jp/bunka/bunkabunkazaitop/bunkazai/sinbunka/28bunkazai.html|title=市原市の新cultural property（平成28th year 度）|work=[[市原市]]|date=2017-06-30|accessdate=2020-05-19}} == Overview == The Inaridai Kofun Group consists of 12 kofun, none of which are large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. This site was excavated during 1976 and 1977. The No.1 Kofun, from which the silver-inlaid sword was excavated, is a circular tomb approximately 28 meters in diameter. It was constructed after No.2 and No.3 kofun. On the southern bank of the Yōrō River lies the {{ill|Anegasaki Futatsuzuka Kofun|ja|姉崎二子塚古墳|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Futatsuzuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}, a contemporaneous Kofun measuring 103 meters in total length, identified as the tomb of the chief of {{ill|Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上海上国造|1=Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}}. The size and grave goods of the No.1 Kofun suggest the interred individual was a warrior. burial goods from this site date back to the mid to late 5th century. The "Ō" mentioned in the sword inscription is widely debated, but the leading theory identifies this as a ruler from the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト政権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kingship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2658252}}. This provides important evidence of the Yamato administration's direct governance over warriors in the {{ill|Eastern Provinces|ja|東国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Provinces|1=Tōgoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10343838}}. A similar example is the {{ill|Inariyama Sword|lt=gold-inlaid iron sword|ja|金錯銘鉄剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Sword|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷山古墳出土鉄剣|1=Inariyama Sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q856395}} excavated from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Kofun|1=Inariyama Kofun (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11596864}} in {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|qid=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama|1=Gyōda}}. == burial and burial goods == The tomb contains two burial facilities: the central wooden coffin and the northern wooden coffin, suggesting two warriors were buried sequentially. * The central coffin contained three iron swords, one riveted {{ill|Tankō|en|Tankō|qid=Q1687003|lt=Tankō|1=Tankō}}, three iron arrowheads, and one knife. * The northern coffin contained one iron sword, ten iron arrowheads, a quiver fitting set, and other items. The riveted cuirass from the central coffin is dated to the mid-5th century, while the quiver fittings from the northern coffin suggest the late 5th century. == Inscription == [[File:「王賜」銘鉄剣 (複製).JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Replica of the "Ōshi" inscribed iron sword}}{{small|On display at the Ichihara City Historic Site Center.}}]] {{main|{{ill|Iron swords and iron sword inscriptions|ja|鉄剣・鉄刀銘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron swords and iron sword inscriptions|qq=}}}}
(Obverse) 王賜□□敬□（安）{{Sfn|Higashino|2010|p=12}} (Reverse) ＿＿此延□□□□ {{Sfn|Higashino|2010|p=12}}
== Interred Individuals == The interred individuals are thought to have been two warriors who served under a "Ō" and were granted the inscribed sword in recognition of their achievements. The "Ō" in the inscription is most likely one of the {{ill|Five kings of Wa|en|Five kings of Wa|qid=Q1152880|lt=Five Kings of Wa|1=Five kings of Wa}}, with the most widely accepted theory identifying "Ō" as {{ill|Sai of Wa|en|Sai of Wa|qid=Q28692299|lt=Sai of Wa|1=Sai of Wa}}, or Emperor Ingyō. However, some speculate that "Ō" refers to the chief interred in the {{ill|Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|ja|姉崎二子塚古墳|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}. == Artifact Return Issue == Some burial goods from Inaridai No.1 Kofun were removed without permission by a male member of the excavation team in the 1990s, allegedly to complete a report. In 2019, 77 of 87 burial goods were recovered via legal enforcement, but 10 remain missing.{{Cite web|和書|author=毎日新聞|title=発掘されたトラブル 1500th year 前の鉄剣 Excavated Artifacts消え、国の文化財指定されず30th year 千葉・市原|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200725/11e/041/040/022000c|work=[[毎日新聞社]]|date=2020-07-25|accessdate=2020-07-26|language=ja}} In December of that year, the city filed a lawsuit for their return.{{Cite web|和書|author=東京新聞|url=https://www.tokyo-np.co.jp/article/528|title=古墳Excavated Artifactsの返還を 市原市が提訴へ 元調査団の70代男性|work=[[東京新聞社]]|date=2019-12-14|accessdate=2020-05-19}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Harashima Reiji|authorlink=原島礼二|title=Landscapes of Ancient Eastern Japan|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|year=1993|isbn=4642073949|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Higashino Haruyuki|authorlink=東野治之|title=The Ancient History of Writing (Revised Edition)|publisher=[[Iwanami Shoten]]|year=2010|isbn=9784000284370|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Inaridai Kofun Group|en|Inaridai Kofun Group|qid=Q11596849|lt=Inaridai Kofun Group|1=Inaridai Kofun Group}} * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Infobox ancient site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kokubunji.html Walking Among the Kofun of Bōsō, Ichihara City Kokubunjidai Kofun] * [https://www.city.ichihara.chiba.jp/maibun/lab.htm Ichihara City Historic Site Research Center] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|稲荷台1号墳|version=104252509|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:稲荷台1号墳]] [[en:Inari Daichi Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11596847}} [[Category:Emperor Ingyō]] [[Category:Epigraphy]] [[Category:History of Ichihara, Chiba]] [[Category:Inscriptions]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
